title: ​​Surrey: Local government reorganisation​
body: "Timeline  5 February 2025  The Minister of State for Local Government and English Devolution wrote to all councils in Surrey, enclosing an invitation to submit a proposal for unitary local government.  Councils in Surrey are working to an accelerated timeline for local government reorganisation as set out in their invitation letter.   17 June 2025  A written ministerial statement was made to Parliament.  This set out that a consultation on proposals for local government reorganisation in Surrey had been launched.  28 October 2025  A written ministerial statement was made to Parliament. This set out the Secretary of State’s decision to implement the proposal for 2 unitary councils in Surrey.  Secondary legislation, which will be subject to Parliamentary approval, will now be taken forward to abolish existing councils, establish new councils and make transitional arrangements.  Accompanying this announcement, letters were sent to:    Surrey council leaders   Surrey council chief executives   Commissioners at Spelthorne Borough Council and Woking Borough Council    "
